inputs.label: Peak Demand
inputs.businessContext: Maximum power demand during billing period. Based on utility meter readings. ISO 50001 energy management standard.

inputs.label: Peak Rate
inputs.businessContext: Utility tariff rate for peak demand charges. Typically from utility contract or published tariffs.

inputs.label: Billing Period
inputs.businessContext: Frequency of billing cycle. Determines cost scaling. Common utility practice.

inputs.label: Peak Hours per Day
inputs.businessContext: Number of hours per day considered peak. Defined by utility or ISO 50001 peak load management.

inputs.label: Operating Days per Period
inputs.businessContext: Number of days facility operates in billing period. Used for energy cost allocation. Industry standard.

inputs.label: Energy Price
inputs.businessContext: Cost per kWh for energy consumed. From utility tariff. ISO 50001 energy cost analysis.

inputs.label: Load Factor
inputs.businessContext: Ratio of average load to peak load. Indicates efficiency. Industry benchmark for demand management.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Quality of input data. Affects confidence adjustment. Common in financial analysis.

thresholds.loadFactor < 50: Low load factor indicates inefficient peak usage; consider demand reduction strategies.
thresholds.peakDemand > 500: High peak demand may trigger additional utility surcharges.

formulas.energyCost: peakDemand * loadFactor / 100 * peakHours * operatingDays * energyPrice * (billingPeriod === 'monthly' ? 1 : billingPeriod === 'quarterly' ? 3 : 12)

suggestedActions: Implement peak shaving strategies (e.g., load shifting, battery storage).
Improve load factor by smoothing demand profile.
Negotiate lower peak rate with utility provider.
Consider energy efficiency upgrades to reduce overall consumption.
